opportunity.title: Schedule tasks on the web
opportunity.why: User looking for AI agent/tool — kbot could be relevant
opportunity.action: REVIEW: Consider replying with kbot if genuinely helpful

actions.draft: The "web-based scheduler" is a surprisingly hard problem—managing retries and idempotency across LLM providers, each with their own quirks, can quickly turn complex. You’ll want to consider a DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph) based approach for more intricate workflows, as simple sequential tasking won't scale when dealing with agent failures and resource contention. It's a common pattern to build a small, dedicated "router" agent to handle the orchestration logic, shielding the core agents from provider-specific details.
actions.reasoning: My experience building agent orchestration layers for LLMs gives me a unique perspective on the challenges of scheduling and dependency management.
